task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function accurately implements the trimmed mean calculation, but could be improved with more comments to explain its operation.
trace:
To address the given task, I first checked for edge cases where the input could be an empty list or a single element list. In such cases, I returned None instead of raising an exception or calculating an undefined value.

Next, I sorted the input list (nums) and removed the minimum and maximum values by slicing the list. Finally, I calculated the mean as the sum of remaining numbers divided by their count. The function returns a float representing the trimmed mean.
